name: Aashirwad Aadtiya
title: Software Developer & AI/ML Engineer
about: I am a final-year B.Tech student specialising in Computer Science and AI/ML at RGPV University, graduating July 2026. I build full-stack applications and production-grade AI pipelines — from a deployed Service Management System for a real client to an LLM-powered repository intelligence agent using Google Gemini and Supabase vector databases. I am comfortable across the full stack (Next.js, FastAPI, MongoDB) and across the AI/ML layer (TensorFlow, PyTorch, n8n, RAG pipelines). I do not just study these technologies — I ship them.

projects.title: Service Management System
projects.description: Production full-stack application built for a real electronics repair business. Manages service logs, inventory, credit settlements, and technician performance. Built with Next.js, MongoDB, and Tailwind CSS.
projects.content:
A real client. A real deployment. A real business running on this application every day.

Features built:
• Service log management with status tracking and technician assignment.
• Credit settlement module with linked transaction history.
• Inventory management with real-time stock updates.
• Expense tracking and assistant salary management with audit logs.
• Real-time dashboard analytics with performance metrics and operational visibility.

Technical decisions:
• Next.js App Router for server-side rendering and fast page loads.
• MongoDB for flexible document storage matching the business's varied service data.
• Tailwind CSS for rapid, consistent UI across dynamic tables and forms.
• Deployed on Vercel with continuous delivery — zero-downtime updates.

This was a solo end-to-end project: requirements, architecture, development, client communication, deployment, and ongoing support.

projects.title: RepoMind AI
projects.description: LLM-powered conversational agent that lets you query any GitHub repository in natural language. Built with n8n, Google Gemini, and Supabase vector DB.
projects.content:
RepoMind AI is an end-to-end automated GenAI pipeline built entirely from scratch — no boilerplate frameworks, no tutorials.

How it works:
• An n8n automation pipeline fetches and parses any GitHub repository on demand via webhook trigger.
• Repository content is chunked, embedded using Google Gemini embeddings, and stored in a Supabase vector database.
• A multi-step prompt workflow powers a conversational AI agent that answers natural language questions about the codebase with full context retrieval.

Technical challenges solved:
• Designed custom chunking logic to handle context-window constraints during embedding.
• Managed webhook reliability and pipeline retry logic for large repositories.
• Optimised vector retrieval accuracy through embedding strategy iteration.

This project is a direct implementation of production RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) architecture — the same pattern used in enterprise AI assistants.

projects.title: Multimodal Document Intelligence Pipeline
projects.description: A dual-stream (vision + semantic) Document AI pipeline that converts complex, multi-column documents into structured, LLM-ready output. Built with Python, FastAPI, docTR, and TensorFlow.
projects.content:
Most OCR tools treat documents as flat text. This pipeline treats them as structured spatial objects.

Core architecture:
• Dual-stream processing: a vision stream (docTR) captures layout and spatial relationships; a semantic stream reconstructs meaning and reading order.
• Spatial segmentation module using coordinate binning and adaptive y-axis heuristics restores logical reading order across multi-column and irregular layouts.
• Post-OCR normalisation layer reconstructs fragmented tokens into semantically coherent Markdown blocks ready for downstream LLM consumption.
• Coordinate-to-JSON transformation pipeline preserves table structure fidelity.

Results:
• ~90% improvement in table structure fidelity over baseline Tesseract extraction.
• Reliable parsing of complex financial, legal, and technical documents.

Served via FastAPI with a clean REST interface, deployed with CI/CD on Vercel.

projects.title: Network Traffic Analyzer
projects.description: Real-time Java application that captures live network packets, classifies encrypted HTTPS traffic via DNS and TLS SNI parsing, and generates exportable bandwidth analytics.
projects.content:
Built to solve a personal curiosity: what is actually running on my network, and how much bandwidth is each application consuming?

Core capabilities:
• Live packet capture from network interfaces using Pcap4J.
• Domain identification via DNS parsing and TLS SNI extraction — classifies encrypted HTTPS traffic without decryption.
• Bidirectional flow tracking system associates packets with originating application domains.
• CDN traffic aggregation identifies application-level bandwidth usage (YouTube, Google services, etc.).
• Exportable CSV reports with domain hit counts, bandwidth totals, and transfer speeds using IP-to-domain caching and flow mapping.

Engineering highlights:
• Handles high-volume packet streams without dropping data using efficient flow key design.
• TLS SNI extraction works on encrypted traffic — no man-in-the-middle required.

Built entirely in Java using OOP design patterns with clean separation between capture, analysis, and reporting layers.
